relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: This is the record of the human line, born from the Divine image and carried into time.
Male and female together bore the name Human, blessed by the Divine from the beginning.
Adam passed image and likeness into Seth, and the human story continued through generation.
Life widened beyond the named son, with sons and daughters filling the human line.
Adam lived many days, and then the mortality spoken outside Eden became real: he died.
Seth lived and fathered Enosh, the fragile mortal human.
Seth continued in years, and life multiplied through sons and daughters.
Seth’s days completed, and he too passed through death.
Enosh lived and fathered Kenan, and the line continued.
Enosh’s life widened through more sons and daughters.
Enosh’s days completed, and the mortal rhythm continued.
Kenan lived and fathered Mahalalel.
The line widened again through sons and daughters.
Kenan’s days completed, and he died.
Mahalalel lived and fathered Jared.
Mahalalel’s life continued, and the human family expanded.
His days completed, and he died.
Jared lived and fathered Enoch, whose path would interrupt the pattern.
Jared continued in years, and the line widened.
Jared’s days completed, and he died.
Enoch lived and fathered Methuselah.
After fathering Methuselah, Enoch walked with the Divine, living in companionship rather than mere duration.
Enoch’s days were fewer than the others, but his life opened into mystery.
Enoch walked with the Divine, and he was not found, for the Divine took him beyond the ordinary death pattern.
Methuselah lived and fathered Lamech.
Methuselah’s life widened through sons and daughters.
His long days completed, and he died.
Lamech lived and fathered a son whose name would carry the longing for relief.
He named him Noah, hoping this child would bring comfort from the painful toil of cursed ground.
Lamech lived on, and the human line continued.
Lamech’s days completed, and he died.
Noah fathered Shem, Ham, and Japheth, and the story prepared to pass through waters of judgment and renewal.
